creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
I think this should be reconsidered as 'make showconfig' has 2 use cases.

1. Show me what options *I* selected
2. Show me, without changing, what options *are available and their descriptions*

IMHO in neither case am I asking for validation that they are correct since I am saying _show_ not _check_.

It is impossible to 'make showconfig' in a dry-run sense because of this change. You must modify the options before viewing them.

[example requires uncommitted patch to change OPTIONS_RADIO to OPTIONS_SINGLE in www/nginx]
# poudriere options -s www/nginx
[00:00:00] Working on options directory: /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/options
[00:00:00] Using ports from: /root/git/ports/main
[00:00:00] Appending to make.conf: /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/make.conf
====> You must select one and only one option from the PCRE single
=====> No option was selected (and one must be)
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make: stopped in /root/git/ports/main/www/nginx
